summary: Fair‑sounding FM soundboard captures a strong second set highlighted by a scorching Scarlet → Fire jam, though pitch glitches and occasional off‑night moments temper the experience.
review: The show shines in its second set – the Scarlet/Fire transition is described as magnificent and Jerry’s solo work is praised as “on fire.”  The opening Shakedown and a ripping Minglewood Blues also stand out.  However, the FM‑SBD source suffers from pitch errors, a missing portion of Fire and a dry, sometimes thin sound that keeps the recording quality at only a fair level.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rave about the performance (especially the second‑set jam and Jerry’s solo) while one detailed review notes pitch and cut issues that keep the recording from being excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cleanest FM‑SBD available but has noticeable pitch errors (-1% to -1.5% depending on track), a cut segment of Fire, dry mids and reduced high‑end in some versions.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is uneven with a few weak spots, but the second set delivers a solid performance highlighted by the Scarlet/Fire jam and strong drums‑>space segment.  The third set feels ragged, with several songs out of sync.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s solo on Fire is repeatedly called “fire” and his improvisational drive is a high point of the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums>Space sequence is praised as the best part of the set, showing solid playing.
